div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p:
A tax position with uncertain
sustainability

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
STEP 1 -- RECOGNITION:
Is it MORE LIKELY THAN NOT (>50%) the
position is SUSTAINED on its technical
merits, assuming examination?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Recognize NO benefit.
Record the full liability for the
unrecognized tax benefit (UTB). Stop

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
STEP 2 -- MEASUREMENT:
Recognize the LARGEST benefit that is
>50% likely on a CUMULATIVE-probability
basis to be realized on settlement

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Build outcomes with probabilities;
accumulate from the largest benefit down
until cumulative prob > 50%.
That benefit is recognized; the rest is the UTB
